title: Complimentary Sign Engineering | Wholesale | Sunlite
metaDescription: Complimentary engineering services for wholesale sign orders. Structural calculations, electrical layouts, and permit-ready drawings included. 3-week production.
heroSubtitle: Every wholesale sign order includes full engineering services at no additional cost. Structural calculations, electrical layouts, and permit-ready drawings from our German-trained engineering team.

sections.heading: Full Engineering Services Included with Every Order
sections.content:
At Sunlite Signs, complimentary engineering is not a marketing gimmick — it is a core part of our wholesale business model. Every sign order includes comprehensive engineering services performed by our in-house team, covering structural analysis, electrical design, and permit-ready documentation. This service eliminates one of the biggest overhead costs for sign shops: outsourcing engineering to third-party firms at $500-$2,000 per project.

Our engineering department is staffed with professionals trained in German precision manufacturing methods. They bring a systematic approach to every project, ensuring that structural calculations, material selections, and fabrication details are optimized for both performance and manufacturability. The result is a sign that installs cleanly, performs reliably, and passes inspection without callbacks.

For sign shops, this means you can quote projects with full engineering included in your price — a competitive advantage that many shops using other manufacturers cannot match. Your clients get a professionally engineered product, and your shop avoids the cost and delays of outsourced engineering.

sections.heading: What Our Engineering Services Cover
sections.content:
Sunlite complimentary engineering services cover the full scope of technical requirements for any sign project:

  • Structural analysis including wind load calculations per ASCE 7 standards for your specific project location and exposure category
  • Foundation design with pier dimensions, reinforcement specifications, and concrete requirements for freestanding signs
  • Attachment details for wall-mounted signs, including substrate analysis and hardware specifications
  • Electrical layouts with circuit sizing, wire routing, and disconnect specifications
  • Permit-ready drawing packages formatted to meet the requirements of local building departments

All engineering documents are stamped and sealed by a licensed professional engineer when required by your jurisdiction. We maintain PE relationships in all 50 states, so regardless of your project location, we can provide the documentation needed for permit approval.

sections.heading: How to Take Advantage of Complimentary Engineering
sections.content:
Getting started with Sunlite engineering services is straightforward. When you submit a project, include your site survey data, local wind speed requirements, and any specific code requirements from your building department. Our team will generate a complete engineering package alongside your shop drawings, typically within 48 hours of receiving your project specifications.

If you are in the early stages of a project and need engineering support for bidding purposes, our team can provide preliminary structural information and budget-level pricing to help you prepare competitive proposals. This pre-sale engineering support is part of our commitment to helping trade partners win more business with the confidence that every project will be engineered correctly from day one.

faqs.question: Is engineering really included at no cost?
faqs.answer: Yes. Structural calculations, electrical layouts, and permit-ready drawings are included with every wholesale sign order at no additional charge. PE-stamped drawings are also included when required by your local jurisdiction.

faqs.question: Can you provide engineering before I place an order?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer preliminary engineering support during the bidding phase to help you prepare accurate proposals. This includes general structural guidance and budget-level specifications that you can use for estimating purposes.

faqs.question: Do you have PE stamps for all states?
faqs.answer: We maintain licensed Professional Engineer relationships in all 50 states. Regardless of your project location, we can provide PE-stamped engineering documents when required by local building authorities.

faqs.question: How long does it take to receive engineering documents?
faqs.answer: Engineering packages are typically completed within 48 hours of receiving your project specifications and site data. Complex projects with unusual structural requirements may require additional time, which we communicate upfront.

faqs.question: What information do you need from me to start engineering?
faqs.answer: We need your sign dimensions, mounting type, installation location (for wind speed determination), site survey data, and any specific code requirements from your local building department. Our project intake form covers all required information.

title: Structural Engineering for Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite
metaDescription: Structural engineering for signs including wind load analysis, foundation design, and attachment details. Complimentary with wholesale orders. PE stamps available.
heroSubtitle: Professional structural engineering for every sign type. Wind load calculations, foundation specifications, and attachment details included with your wholesale order.

sections.heading: Why Structural Engineering Matters for Signage
sections.content:
Structural engineering is the foundation of safe, code-compliant signage. Every exterior sign must withstand the wind loads, seismic forces, and dead loads specific to its installation location. Sunlite Signs provides complimentary structural engineering with every wholesale order, performed by engineers trained in German precision methods who understand both the physics and the practical realities of sign construction.

Building departments increasingly require engineered drawings for sign permits, particularly for freestanding pylons, large wall-mounted signs, and any sign installed in high-wind zones. Without proper engineering, sign shops face permit delays, costly revisions, and potential liability if a sign fails in service. Our structural engineering eliminates these risks by ensuring every sign meets the requirements of ASCE 7 and applicable local building codes.

For sign shops, outsourcing structural engineering to third-party firms typically costs $500-$2,000 per project and adds days or weeks to the timeline. By including engineering in our wholesale pricing, we remove this cost and delay from your workflow, letting you quote and deliver projects faster.

sections.heading: Our Structural Engineering Process
sections.content:
Sunlite structural engineering follows a systematic process for every project:

  • Site analysis: We determine the basic wind speed, exposure category, and topographic factors for your specific installation location using ASCE 7 criteria
  • Load calculations: Wind loads, dead loads, and live loads are calculated for the specific sign dimensions and mounting configuration
  • Member sizing: Structural members including poles, brackets, and framing are sized to meet required safety factors
  • Connection design: Bolt patterns, weld specifications, and attachment hardware are detailed for the specific installation substrate
  • Foundation specifications: For freestanding signs, we provide pier dimensions, reinforcement schedules, and concrete specifications

All structural packages include calculations and drawings formatted for building department submission. When PE stamps are required, we coordinate with our network of licensed Professional Engineers to provide sealed documents for your specific state.

sections.heading: Common Structural Challenges We Solve
sections.content:
Our engineering team handles the structural challenges that make sign projects complex. High-wind coastal installations require detailed exposure analysis and often mandate higher safety factors. Rooftop signs need structural evaluation of the existing building to verify it can support the additional load. Large pylon signs in seismic zones require combined wind and seismic analysis per current building codes.

We also engineer solutions for challenging mounting substrates. EIFS-clad buildings, metal panel facades, and older masonry structures each require specific attachment strategies to safely support sign loads. Our engineers specify the correct anchoring systems, backing plates, and load distribution methods for each substrate type, giving your installation crew clear direction and reducing field improvisation.

faqs.question: What building codes do your structural calculations follow?
faqs.answer: Our structural calculations are based on ASCE 7 (Minimum Design Loads for Buildings and Other Structures) and the applicable edition of the International Building Code adopted by your local jurisdiction. We verify the specific code edition required before starting calculations.

faqs.question: Can you engineer signs for hurricane zones?
faqs.answer: Yes. We regularly engineer signs for high-wind coastal areas with basic wind speeds exceeding 150 mph. Our calculations account for the increased wind exposure, risk category, and importance factors required by ASCE 7 for these locations.

faqs.question: Do you provide foundation engineering for freestanding signs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every freestanding sign order includes foundation specifications with pier dimensions, depth, reinforcement schedules, and concrete specifications. Soil-specific modifications can be made when geotechnical reports are available.

faqs.question: How do I know if my project requires PE-stamped drawings?
faqs.answer: PE-stamp requirements vary by jurisdiction. Most states require PE-stamped structural drawings for freestanding signs over a certain height or wall-mounted signs exceeding specific size thresholds. Check with your local building department or let us know your location and we will advise.

title: Electrical Engineering for Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite
metaDescription: Electrical engineering for illuminated signs. UL 48 compliant circuit design, power supply sizing, and wiring layouts included with wholesale orders from Sunlite Signs.
heroSubtitle: UL 48 compliant electrical engineering for every illuminated sign. Circuit design, power supply sizing, and wiring details included with your wholesale order.

sections.heading: Electrical Engineering for Illuminated Signage
sections.content:
Proper electrical engineering is critical for illuminated sign safety, code compliance, and long-term reliability. Sunlite Signs includes comprehensive electrical engineering with every wholesale illuminated sign order, ensuring that power supplies are correctly sized, circuits are properly loaded, and wiring meets UL 48 standards.

Electrical issues are one of the most common causes of sign failure and service callbacks. Undersized power supplies, overloaded circuits, and improper wiring connections lead to premature LED failure, tripped breakers, and in worst cases, fire hazards. Our electrical engineering eliminates these problems by designing the complete electrical system before fabrication begins.

Every Sunlite illuminated sign ships fully wired and tested, with UL 48 labels applied. The electrical engineering documentation that accompanies each sign includes circuit diagrams, power supply specifications, and connection details that your installation crew needs to connect the sign to building power safely and in compliance with the National Electrical Code.

sections.heading: Electrical Design Standards and Practices
sections.content:
Our electrical engineering follows UL 48 standards and NEC requirements for sign circuits. Key elements of our electrical design process include:

  • Power supply sizing with appropriate derating factors to prevent overloading and extend component life
  • Circuit layout with balanced loading across multiple power supplies for larger signs
  • Wire gauge selection based on circuit length and load to minimize voltage drop
  • Surge protection integrated into power supply specifications for field reliability
  • Disconnect requirements specified per NEC Article 600 for sign circuits

We also address the practical aspects of field installation. Our electrical packages specify the service requirements (circuit amperage, voltage, and phase) that the electrical contractor needs to provide at the sign location. This coordination between sign installation and electrical rough-in prevents the delays that occur when service capacity or location does not match the sign requirements.

sections.heading: UL 48 Compliance and Safety
sections.content:
UL 48 is the safety standard for electric signs, and compliance is a non-negotiable requirement for professional sign installations. Sunlite Signs is a UL 48 listed manufacturer, meaning our production facility, materials, and processes are regularly audited to ensure ongoing compliance. Every illuminated sign we produce carries a UL label, giving you and your clients the assurance that the product meets the industry's highest safety standard.

For sign shops, UL 48 compliance matters because building inspectors look for the UL label during final inspection. Signs without UL listing can be rejected, creating costly delays and potential re-fabrication. By partnering with Sunlite, you eliminate this risk entirely — every illuminated sign ships with proper UL documentation ready for inspection.

faqs.question: Are all your illuminated signs UL 48 listed?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every illuminated sign manufactured by Sunlite Signs is produced under our UL 48 listing and ships with a UL label. Our facility undergoes regular UL audits to maintain compliance with current safety standards.

faqs.question: What voltage do your signs operate on?
faqs.answer: Standard signs operate on 120V or 277V single-phase power. Our power supplies accept a range of input voltages, and we specify the correct service requirements based on the sign's total electrical load and the available building power.

faqs.question: Do I need a separate electrical permit for sign installation?
faqs.answer: Most jurisdictions require an electrical permit for illuminated sign connections, separate from the sign permit. Our electrical documentation includes the specifications your electrician needs for the electrical permit application.

faqs.question: How do you handle multi-sign installations?
faqs.answer: For projects with multiple illuminated signs, we provide a combined electrical package that coordinates circuit loading, disconnect placement, and service requirements across all signs. This simplifies the electrical rough-in and ensures adequate capacity for the complete installation.

title: Sign Design to Manufacturing | Wholesale | Sunlite
metaDescription: From design concept to finished sign in 3 weeks. Learn how Sunlite Signs' German-engineered manufacturing process delivers precision wholesale signage for the trade.
heroSubtitle: A transparent look at how your project moves from concept to finished product. German-engineered processes, 3-week production, and quality controls at every stage.

sections.heading: From Your Design to a Finished Sign
sections.content:
Understanding how a wholesale sign manufacturer operates helps you set accurate expectations with your clients and plan your project timelines with confidence. At Sunlite Signs, our design-to-manufacturing process follows a structured German-engineered workflow that ensures every sign meets specifications, passes quality inspection, and ships on schedule.

The process begins when you submit your project requirements. This can be as simple as a napkin sketch with dimensions or as detailed as a complete design file with material specifications. Our engineering team reviews every submission and produces detailed shop drawings that cover dimensions, materials, finishes, electrical specifications, and mounting details. These drawings go to you for review and approval before any fabrication begins.

This approval step is critical. It ensures you and your client agree on every detail before we cut material, which virtually eliminates costly mid-production changes and the disputes that come with them. Once you approve the shop drawings, your sign enters our production queue with a standard 3-week timeline to completion.

sections.heading: Production Stages and Quality Controls
sections.content:
Sunlite manufacturing follows a sequential production workflow with quality checkpoints at each stage:

  • Material preparation: Aluminum, steel, acrylic, and other materials are cut and formed to shop drawing dimensions using CNC equipment
  • Fabrication: Components are welded, fastened, and assembled into the sign structure with jig-controlled alignment
  • Electrical assembly: LED modules, power supplies, and wiring are installed and tested per UL 48 requirements
  • Finishing: Paint, powder coat, or vinyl finishes are applied in our controlled environment spray booth
  • Quality inspection: Dimensional verification, illumination testing, and UL compliance checks before crating

Every sign passes through a final quality inspection before it is cleared for shipping. This includes verifying all dimensions against the approved shop drawing, testing illumination for uniform brightness, and confirming that all UL labels and documentation are in place. Signs that do not pass inspection are corrected before shipping — we do not pass quality issues to your installation crew.

sections.heading: Shipping and Installation Support
sections.content:
Sunlite Signs ships completed products via common carrier, with crating designed to protect the sign during transit. Large or fragile signs — from illuminated logo boxes to LED light box signs — are custom-crated with internal bracing to prevent damage. We coordinate shipping schedules with your installation timeline so signs arrive when your crew is ready, not weeks early taking up warehouse space.

Each shipment includes installation documentation with mounting details, electrical connection points, and any special handling instructions. If your crew encounters questions during installation, our support team is available by phone to provide guidance. This after-sale support is part of our commitment to making the wholesale relationship work smoothly from order through final installation.

faqs.question: How detailed do my project files need to be?
faqs.answer: We accept project submissions at any level of detail, from rough sketches with dimensions to complete design files. Our engineering team will produce professional shop drawings regardless of the starting point. More detailed submissions may reduce the time needed for shop drawing preparation.

faqs.question: Can I make changes after approving shop drawings?
faqs.answer: Changes after shop drawing approval may be possible depending on the production stage. Early changes before material cutting are generally straightforward. Changes after fabrication has begun may require additional time and cost. Contact your project coordinator as early as possible if changes are needed.

faqs.question: How are signs shipped?
faqs.answer: Signs ship via common carrier on pallets or in custom crates depending on size and fragility. We use LTL freight for most orders and can coordinate with your preferred carrier if needed. Shipping costs are quoted separately based on destination and sign dimensions.

faqs.question: Do you offer installation services?
faqs.answer: As a wholesale manufacturer, we do not provide installation services directly. We supply your sign shop with complete installation documentation, and our support team is available by phone if your crew has questions during the install process.

title: Sign Installation Planning | Wholesale | Sunlite
metaDescription: Comprehensive sign installation planning resources for trade professionals. Mounting details, electrical coordination, and site preparation guides from Sunlite Signs.
heroSubtitle: Set your installation crew up for success with detailed planning resources. Mounting specifications, electrical coordination, and site preparation — all included with your Sunlite order.

sections.heading: Planning for a Smooth Sign Installation
sections.content:
A well-planned installation is the difference between a profitable job and a callback nightmare. Sunlite Signs supports your installation process with detailed planning documentation included with every wholesale order, covering mounting specifications, electrical coordination requirements, and site preparation checklists that ensure your crew arrives on site fully prepared.

Installation planning begins during the engineering phase. When our team designs the structural and electrical systems for your sign, they simultaneously develop the installation documentation your crew will need in the field. This includes mounting hardware specifications, required tools and equipment, substrate preparation details, and electrical connection instructions specific to each sign.

For sign shops, thorough installation planning translates directly to profitability. When crews have clear instructions and the right hardware, installations go faster, callbacks decrease, and your labor costs stay predictable. Sunlite engineering supports this outcome by thinking through installation logistics during the design phase, not as an afterthought.

sections.heading: Key Installation Planning Elements
sections.content:
Every Sunlite sign ships with installation documentation that covers these critical planning elements:

  • Mounting hardware specifications: Bolt sizes, anchor types, and fastener torque values for the specific installation substrate
  • Crane and lift requirements: Sign weight and center of gravity information for lift planning on large installations
  • Electrical rough-in coordinates: Exact locations for power supply connections, disconnects, and conduit penetrations
  • Foundation and embedment details: For freestanding signs, pier dimensions, anchor bolt templates, and concrete specifications
  • Clearance requirements: Minimum clearances for pedestrian and vehicle traffic per ADA and local code

We also provide pre-installation checklists that your project managers can use to verify site readiness before dispatching a crew. Confirming that foundations are cured, electrical service is available, and access equipment is scheduled prevents the wasted trips that erode installation margins.

faqs.question: Do you provide installation instructions with every sign?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every Sunlite sign ships with project-specific installation documentation including mounting details, hardware specifications, electrical connection points, and any special handling instructions relevant to the specific sign type and size.

faqs.question: Can you help coordinate crane requirements?
faqs.answer: We provide sign weight, dimensions, and center of gravity information that your crane operator needs for lift planning. For complex installations, our engineering team can review your rigging plan and provide guidance on lift point locations.

faqs.question: How far in advance should I plan the installation?
faqs.answer: We recommend coordinating installation logistics as soon as shop drawings are approved. This gives adequate time to schedule electrical rough-in, foundation work, access equipment, and permits. Our 3-week production timeline provides a natural planning window.

faqs.question: What if my crew encounters issues during installation?
faqs.answer: Our technical support team is available by phone during business hours to assist with installation questions. For complex installations, we can arrange a pre-installation call with our engineering team to walk through the installation sequence with your crew leader.

faqs.question: Do you provide anchor bolt templates?
faqs.answer: Yes. For freestanding signs, we provide full-scale anchor bolt templates and foundation detail drawings that your concrete contractor uses to set the foundation before the sign arrives. Accurate templates are critical for a proper fit.

title: Sign Maintenance Guide | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Professional sign maintenance guide for trade partners. LED service, cleaning procedures, and preventive maintenance schedules for Sunlite wholesale signage products.
heroSubtitle: Keep your installed signs performing at their best. Maintenance procedures, service schedules, and troubleshooting guidance for Sunlite sign products.

sections.heading: Maintaining Commercial Signage for Long-Term Performance
sections.content:
Sign maintenance is an often-overlooked revenue stream for sign shops and a critical factor in keeping your clients' signage looking professional year after year. Sunlite Signs designs every product with serviceability in mind, using accessible component layouts, standard hardware, and modular construction that makes routine maintenance efficient for your service crews.

Regular maintenance extends sign life, preserves appearance, and prevents the small issues that escalate into expensive emergency repairs. For your clients, a well-maintained sign projects professionalism and protects their investment. For your shop, maintenance contracts provide predictable recurring revenue and strengthen client relationships that lead to future sign projects.

This guide covers the key maintenance practices for Sunlite sign products, including LED illumination service, exterior cleaning procedures, and structural inspection protocols. Following these practices helps ensure that every sign you install continues to perform as designed for years after installation.

sections.heading: Maintenance Procedures by Component
sections.content:
Effective sign maintenance addresses each major component on an appropriate schedule:

  • LED modules: Inspect annually for failed or dim modules. Sunlite LED systems use plug-in module connections for fast field replacement without soldering. Keep spare modules on hand for common sign types in your service area.
  • Power supplies: Check operation annually. Listen for buzzing or humming that indicates a failing supply. Sunlite power supplies are mounted with quick-connect wiring for efficient replacement.
  • Sign faces: Clean semi-annually using mild soap and water. Avoid abrasive cleaners or pressure washers that can damage face materials and graphics. Inspect for cracks, yellowing, or delamination.
  • Cabinet and structure: Inspect annually for corrosion, paint damage, loose fasteners, and seal integrity. Touch up paint damage promptly to prevent corrosion spread on metal components.
  • Electrical connections: Inspect annually for loose terminals, damaged wiring, and proper grounding. Tighten connections and replace any damaged wiring per NEC requirements.

Sunlite provides detailed maintenance documentation with each sign, including component part numbers and ordering information for replacement parts. This makes it easy for your service team to identify and order the exact parts needed for any maintenance task.

faqs.question: How often should commercial signs be maintained?
faqs.answer: We recommend a minimum annual inspection and maintenance visit for illuminated signs. High-traffic or coastal locations may benefit from semi-annual service. Exterior cleaning should be performed at least twice per year to maintain appearance.

faqs.question: Can I order replacement parts for Sunlite signs?
faqs.answer: Yes. We maintain inventory of replacement components including LED modules, power supplies, face panels, and hardware. Each sign ships with documentation listing the specific part numbers installed, making reordering straightforward.

faqs.question: How do I troubleshoot a partially dark sign?
faqs.answer: A partially dark sign typically indicates failed LED modules or a power supply issue. Check the power supply for output first. If the supply is functioning, inspect individual LED modules in the dark section. Sunlite LED systems use parallel wiring so a single module failure does not affect other modules.

faqs.question: What cleaning products are safe for sign faces?
faqs.answer: Use mild soap and water with a soft cloth or sponge. Avoid ammonia-based cleaners, abrasive pads, and pressure washers. For polycarbonate faces, use cleaners specifically formulated for polycarbonate to avoid micro-scratching that causes hazing over time.

title: Sign Warranty Coverage | Wholesale | Sunlite
metaDescription: Comprehensive warranty coverage on wholesale signage from Sunlite Signs. LED, structural, and finish warranties explained. German-engineered quality you can stand behind.
heroSubtitle: Stand behind every sign you sell with Sunlite warranty coverage. Comprehensive protection on LED components, structural integrity, and finishes gives your clients confidence.

sections.heading: Warranty Coverage That Protects Your Reputation
sections.content:
When you install a sign under your company name, your reputation is on the line. Sunlite Signs backs every wholesale product with comprehensive warranty coverage that gives you and your clients confidence in the product's long-term performance. Our warranty program is structured to support sign shops with straightforward claims processing and rapid replacement part delivery.

Our German-engineered manufacturing processes and rigorous quality controls minimize warranty issues in the first place. Every sign undergoes a complete quality inspection before shipping, including dimensional verification, illumination testing, and UL compliance checks. This proactive approach to quality means fewer field failures, fewer callbacks, and lower warranty claim rates compared to manufacturers that rely on field corrections to catch production issues.

For sign shops, strong manufacturer warranty coverage is a selling tool. When you can tell your client that the sign is backed by a factory warranty covering LED components, structural integrity, and finishes, it adds value to your proposal and differentiates your offering from competitors who sell unbranded or lightly warranted products.

sections.heading: What Our Warranty Covers
sections.content:
Sunlite Signs warranty coverage is structured by component to reflect the different performance expectations of each sign element:

  • LED modules and power supplies: 5-year warranty against manufacturing defects and premature lumen depreciation below the L70 maintenance threshold
  • Structural components: 10-year warranty on welded aluminum and steel structural members against failure under designed load conditions
  • Paint and powder coat finishes: 5-year warranty against peeling, flaking, and excessive fading under normal outdoor exposure
  • Electrical wiring and connections: 5-year warranty on all internal wiring and connection points against manufacturing defects
  • Face materials: 3-year warranty on polycarbonate and acrylic face panels against cracking and excessive yellowing under normal conditions

Warranty coverage begins on the date of shipment and applies to the original installation. Claims are processed through your Sunlite trade account, and replacement parts typically ship within 5 business days of an approved claim. Our goal is to get your service team the parts they need quickly so issues are resolved before your client relationship is affected.

sections.heading: Filing a Warranty Claim
sections.content:
Filing a warranty claim with Sunlite Signs is designed to be straightforward for trade partners. Contact your account representative or submit a claim through our trade portal with the sign serial number, a description of the issue, and photos if applicable. Our warranty team reviews claims promptly and responds with a resolution within 48 hours.

For clear-cut component failures like a dead LED module or a failed power supply, we often approve replacement parts immediately based on the sign's production records without requiring return of the defective component. This expedited process gets parts to your crew faster and minimizes the time your client's sign is underperforming. For more complex claims, our technical team may request additional diagnostic information to identify the root cause and ensure the correct replacement parts are sent.

faqs.question: How long is the warranty on LED components?
faqs.answer: LED modules and power supplies carry a 5-year warranty against manufacturing defects and premature lumen depreciation below the L70 threshold. Extended warranty options are available for projects that require longer coverage periods.

faqs.question: How do I file a warranty claim?
faqs.answer: Contact your account representative or submit a claim through the Sunlite trade portal. Include the sign serial number, issue description, and photos. Our warranty team typically responds within 48 hours with a resolution.

faqs.question: Do I need to return defective parts?
faqs.answer: For common component failures, we typically approve replacement parts without requiring return of the defective component. In some cases, we may request a defective part for quality analysis purposes, in which case we provide a prepaid shipping label.

faqs.question: Does the warranty cover installation damage?
faqs.answer: Our warranty covers manufacturing defects, not damage caused during installation or by external factors such as vehicle impacts, vandalism, or extreme weather events beyond designed load conditions. We recommend documenting sign condition at installation for reference.

faqs.question: Can I offer extended warranties to my clients?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer extended warranty programs that allow sign shops to provide longer coverage periods to their end clients. Contact your account representative for extended warranty pricing and terms.
